testSteps.description: Given I have test aggregate properties with id "test-aggregate-id", name "Test Aggregate", and version 1
And I have test passport with userId "test-user-id" and permissions ["read", "write"]
When I create a test aggregate with the properties and passport
Then the aggregate should have id "test-aggregate-id"

children.description: Test Actor ensures that "test-aggregate-id" does equal "test-aggregate-id"

testSteps.description: And the aggregate should have name "Test Aggregate"

children.description: Test Actor ensures that "Test Aggregate" does equal "Test Aggregate"

testSteps.description: And the aggregate passport should match the provided passport

children.description: Test Actor ensures that { userId: "test-user-id", permissions: [ "read", "write" ] } does equal { userId: "test-user-id", permissions: [ "read", "write" ] }

testSteps.description: And the aggregate should not be deleted initially

children.description: Test Actor ensures that false does equal false

testSteps.description: And the aggregate should have no domain events initially

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 0 does equal 0

testSteps.description: And the aggregate should have no integration events initially

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 0 does equal 0

userStory.narrative:
As a domain developer
I want to use aggregate roots to manage domain and integration events
So that I can build robust domain models with event sourcing capabilities
